scenario_title: Avoiding hints to preserve mastery streak during individualized practice
minimal_context: The student is working individually on math exercises on a computer in the lab, trying to achieve five correct answers in a row.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please continue working individually on your math exercises on the computer.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looking at the computer screen) I am trying to get five-in-a-row, but I just got this answer wrong. I'm going to try using the same strategy again for the next problem.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I got it wrong again. I don't want to click the hint button because it will automatically give me an X.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm wary of using the embedded hints because it interrupts my accumulation of check marks and I'll have to rebuild back towards five-in-a-row. Can you just help me with it so I don't lose my points?

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please continue working individually on your math exercises on the computer.

STUDENT: (Looking at the computer screen) I am trying to get five-in-a-row, but I just got this answer wrong. I'm going to try using the same strategy again for the next problem.

TUTOR: Keep trying your best.

STUDENT: I got it wrong again. I don't want to click the hint button because it will automatically give me an X.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: I'm wary of using the embedded hints because it interrupts my accumulation of check marks and I'll have to rebuild back towards five-in-a-row. Can you just help me with it so I don't lose my points?

task.content: doing math exercises, each with different sets of problems

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: If students get the answer wrong
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: continued with the same strategy for a problem or two. However, since they continued getting answers wrong, they soon sought help
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: wary of using the embedded hints for assistance because it meant they would not earn points for that exercise

behavior_patterns.condition: Once a student clicks on the “hint” button, they automatically receive an ✗. This interrupts their accumulation of ✔ marks
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: preferred turning to peers and teachers. The most common first step we observed was to ask a more math-savvy peer for help
behavior_patterns.internal_state: motivating and gave them a sense that they are mastering different procedures
